title: Aeschynanthus asclepioides
taxonomy: Aeschynanthus asclepioides is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This plant belongs to the genus Aeschynanthus, which is native to Southeast Asia. The genus Aeschynanthus is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics. Aeschynanthus asclepioides is specifically classified as an epiphytic evergreen shrub. Its classification helps to identify and distinguish it from other species within the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Aeschynanthus asclepioides, also known as the "Mist Maestro" or "Lord's Wine", is a flowering plant species belonging to the Gesneriaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, Aeschynanthus asclepioides is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aeschynanthus asclepioides is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make patent medicines and herbal tonics.
